text_original: May not the Way (or Dao) of Heaven be compared to the (method of) bending a bow?
text_normalized: May not the Way (or Dao) of Heaven be compared to the (method of) bending a bow?

text_original: The (part of the bow) which was high is brought low, and what was low is raised up.
text_normalized: The (part of the bow) which was high is brought low, and what was low is raised up.

text_original: (So Heaven) diminishes where there is superabundance, and supplements where there is deficiency.
text_normalized: (So Heaven) diminishes where there is superabundance, and supplements where there is deficiency.

text_original: It is the Way of Heaven to diminish superabundance, and to supplement deficiency.
text_normalized: It is the Way of Heaven to diminish superabundance, and to supplement deficiency.

text_original: It is not so with the way of man.
text_normalized: It is not so with the way of man.

text_original: He takes away from those who have not enough to add to his own superabundance.
text_normalized: He takes away from those who have not enough to add to his own superabundance.

text_original: Who can take his own superabundance and therewith serve all under heaven?
text_normalized: Who can take his own superabundance and therewith serve all under heaven?

text_original: Only he who is in possession of the Dao!
text_normalized: Only he who is in possession of the Dao!

text_original: Therefore the (ruling) sage acts without claiming the results as his;
text_normalized: Therefore the (ruling) sage acts without claiming the results as his;

text_original: he achieves his merit and does not rest (arrogantly) in it:
text_normalized: he achieves his merit and does not rest (arrogantly) in it:

text_original: - he does not wish to display his superiority.
text_normalized: - he does not wish to display his superiority.
